title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Lobatse Town Council (general) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Lobatse Town Council (general) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Lobatse Town Council (General) Bye-laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Interpretation

definitions.definition: approved by the council or by any duly authorised officer of the council
any person who carries on the business of selling whether by wholesale or retail, bakery products baked or made by him
any premises on which is carried on any of the processes of or incidental to baking or the manufacture or storage of bakery products for use by persons other than those residing on the premises
bread, biscuits, rolls, tarts, cakes, pies, confectionery or sweetmeats
any premises which are used or intended to be used as a place of trade or industry
any person who sells or exposes for sale or supplies butcher's meat for human consumption
any premises used for the purpose of carrying on the business of a butcher

the flesh or offal of any animal intended for human consumption or any products manufactured therefrom, but does not include canned or potted meats, biltong, ham, sausages, bacon, salted and other prepared meats, fish, poultry or venison
the Lobatse Town Council
any house, room, shed, hut or any other structure or plan whatsoever, any portion whereof is used by any human being for sleeping in or in which any human being dwells
any thing whatsoever (other than drugs or water), in any form, state or stage of preparation, which is ordinarily used or intended to be used for human consumption
any premises which are used or intended to be used as a place for the preparation, manufacture, keeping, storing, depositing, conveying, handling and exposing for sale of food and drink
any room used or intended to be used as a sleeping, living or work room
any building, erection or place adapted or constructed for the use of human beings for the purposes of defecation or urination
any domestic bovine animal, goat, sheep, swine, horse, donkey or mule
any duly appointed official of the council
any fowl, turkey, goose or duck
any building or part thereof, store, shop, tenement or other erection above or below the ground and the land used or occupied in connection therewith
any abattoir, slaughter pole or place set apart for slaughtering livestock, the meat of which is intended for sale
a cowshed, stall, pen or sty

any street, square, road, lane, footpath, pavement, thoroughfare or public place extending in width from the boundary of any lot or area of land and, includes any work or thing forming part of or connected with such street
any discharge of a non-excremental nature from any waste-water fitment, gully trap, grease trap or laundry
